remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Spontaneous resolution by adulthood in majority (persistent adult acne in 12-22% of women, 3% of men); moderate-severe acne rarely self-resolves; scarring occurs in up to 95% without adequate treatment
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Oral isotretinoin: 85-90% achieve sustained remission (acne-free at 5 years) after one course; relapse requiring retreat in 15-20%; topical adapalene + benzoyl peroxide: 65-70% reduction in inflammatory lesions at 12 weeks
remission.gap_size: Large — isotretinoin is highly effective but dramatically underused due to iPLEDGE programme barriers and pregnancy concerns; patients spend years on antibiotics instead
remission.barrier_detail: The iPLEDGE programme creates substantial barriers to isotretinoin access (monthly blood tests, pregnancy tests, pharmacy restrictions); this leads to years of antibiotic courses creating antibiotic resistance and allowing scarring to progress; acne is not considered a 'serious' disease despite causing significant psychological morbidity and permanent scarring
remission.last_soc_change: 2022 — FDA approved clascoterone (Winlevi) — first androgen receptor inhibitor for acne; 2021 Accutane biosimilar access improved; 2019 AAD guidelines updated; isotretinoin approved 1982; tetracycline antibiotics for acne since 1950s
remission.outdated_treatments: Prolonged oral antibiotic monotherapy without topical retinoid co-prescription (antibiotic resistance — P. acnes resistance to tetracyclines increasing; AAD guidelines recommend antibiotic + BPO or retinoid combination, never antibiotic alone); topical antibiotic monotherapy without BPO (same resistance issue); iodine-based products; comedone extractions as sole intervention
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Low-glycaemic diet (RCT data: significant reduction in inflammatory lesion count); dairy reduction (observational data consistent with RCTs); zinc supplementation (equivalent to some antibiotics in trials, particularly for inflammatory acne); spearmint tea (anti-androgenic effect in small RCTs for hormonal acne); niacinamide topical
remission.frontier_signal: Clascoterone (Winlevi — topical anti-androgen without systemic hormonal effects); trifarotene (fourth-generation retinoid for truncal acne); phage therapy targeting C. acnes; microbiome modulation; intense pulsed light (IPL) and photodynamic therapy for recurrence prevention post-isotretinoin
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.
